strengths: Strong project depth — both projects show real architectural thinking, including database normalisation, role-based access control, and agentic AI routing, which is impressive for a junior candidate
Good modern tech stack coverage — Python, Flask, React, Firebase, WebSockets, and multiple AI APIs demonstrate broad and relevant hands-on exposure
Clear and specific project descriptions — bullet points are detailed and outcome-focused rather than vague, making technical contributions easy to assess
weaknesses: No work experience listed — the CV relies entirely on personal projects, which raises questions about professional environment exposure, teamwork, and real-world delivery under constraints
Placeholder contact details and GitHub/LinkedIn links are not filled in, which looks unprofessional and makes the candidate unreachable
The Salary Sorter description appears cut off mid-sentence ('verifying correct beha...'), suggesting the CV was not proofread before submission
missing: Education section with current HNC/HND details and institution
Skills section summarising languages, tools, and frameworks at a glance
Full name and completed contact information
summary: Godwin shows genuine technical ability and initiative for a junior candidate, with two well-described projects that demonstrate real backend and AI integration skills. However, the absence of any work experience, incomplete contact details, and missing education section significantly weaken the CV and must be addressed before submission.
